figures.caption: Figure 1: A motivating example of repository-level code generation under executable validation. The same execution symptom may arise from unmet external dependencies, broken repositoryinternal references, or both.

Table 2: Comparison of repository-level code generation methods on RAL-Bench under different backbone models. Functional and Non-functional denote Functional Correctness and NonFunctional Quality, respectively. Numbers are percentages. Gray numbers in parentheses indicate absolute changes relative to Direct under the same backbone. Red rows report its relative improvement over the best non-EnvGraph baseline.

Table 4: Ablation study of EnvGraph on RAL-Bench under different backbone models. EEBA denotes execution-evidencebased attribution. Gray numbers in parentheses indicate absolute drops relative to the full model under the same backbone.
Figure 4: Example of the external environment graph for repository Client, showing unmet external dependency satisfaction.
Figure 6: Failure-type distribution of EnvGraph across different backbone models.
